methods.name: Stage-Progression

methods.name: Peer-Median

data.narrative:
Parry Labs, a Series B-stage defense and security company, is evaluated against a universe of 10 comparable companies: 2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms. The private peer set includes five named comparables—Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Castelion, and Defense Unicorns—all at identical stage.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $80M total raised as a proxy base and applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $152M. Peer-median analysis examines same-sector, same-stage comparables across the Defense & Security space. The six-company peer median valued at $1.2B (range: $1.0B–$1.5B) reflects current market pricing for this risk profile and sector.

The triangulated range is $129M to $1.4B, with a median of $676M. The wide dispersion reflects material tension between the two methods: stage-progression anchors conservatively to capital invested, while peer-median reflects significantly higher market valuations for direct competitors. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the peer set for Parry Labs' specific technology and addressable market, product maturity relative to comparables, and whether peer valuations reflect recent market repricing in defense-tech funding.
